children.heading: Division of corps; basis of categories
children.label: a Division of corps; basis of categories
children.text: For the purpose of establishing eligibility of officers of the Regular Corps for promotions, the Surgeon General shall by regulation divide the corps into professional categories. Each category shall, as far as practicable, be based upon one of the subjects of examination set forth in or upon a subdivision of such subject, and the categories shall be designed to group officers by fields of training in such manner that officers in any one grade in any one category will be available for similar duty in the discharge of the several functions of the Service.section 209(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Assignment of officers
children.label: b Assignment of officers
children.text: Each officer of the Regular Corps on active duty shall, on the basis of his training and experience, be assigned by the Surgeon General to one of the categories established by regulations under subsection (a). Except upon amendment of such regulations, no assignment so made shall be changed unless the Surgeon General finds (1) that the original assignment was erroneous, or (2) that the officer is equally well qualified to serve in another category to which he has requested to be transferred, and that such transfer is in the interests of the Service.

children.heading: Maximum number of officers in each category
children.label: c Maximum number of officers in each category
children.text: Within the limits fixed by the Secretary in regulations under for any fiscal year, the Surgeon General shall determine for each category in the Regular Corps the maximum number of officers authorized to be in each of the grades from the warrant officer (W–1) grade to the director grade, inclusive.section 207(d) of this title

children.heading: Vacancies in grade for purposes of promotion
children.label: d Vacancies in grade for purposes of promotion
children.text: The excess of the number so fixed for any grade in any category over the number of officers of the Regular Corps on active duty in such grade in such category (including in the case of the director grade, officers holding such grade in accordance with ) shall for the purpose of promotions constitute vacancies in such grade in such category. For purposes of this subsection, an officer who has been temporarily promoted or who is temporarily holding the grade of director in accordance with shall be deemed to hold the grade to which so promoted or which he is temporarily holding; but while he holds such promotion or grade, and while any officer is temporarily assigned to a position pursuant to , the number fixed under subsection (c) of this section for the grade of his permanent rank shall be reduced by one.section 207(c) of this titlesection 207(c) of this titlesection 206(c) of this title

children.heading: Absence of vacancy in grade as affecting promotion
children.label: e Absence of vacancy in grade as affecting promotion
children.text: The absence of a vacancy in a grade in a category shall not prevent an appointment to such grade pursuant to , a permanent length of service promotion, or the recall of a retired officer to active duty; but the making of such an appointment, promotion, or recall shall be deemed to fill a vacancy if one exists.section 209 of this title

children.heading: Vacancy in grade as affecting maximum number for each category
children.label: f Vacancy in grade as affecting maximum number for each category
children.text: Whenever a vacancy exists in any grade in a category the Surgeon General may increase by one the number fixed by him under subsection (c) for the next lower grade in the same category, without regard to the numbers fixed in regulations under ; and in that event the vacancy in the higher grade shall not be filled except by a permanent promotion, and upon the making of such promotion the number for the next lower grade shall be reduced by one.section 207(d) of this title
